title: Clidemia quinquenodis
intro: Clidemia quinquenervia, also known as the Australian bush willow, is a small tree or shrub native to the tropical regions of Australia, Southeast Asia, and the Pacific Islands. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antihistamine properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of allergies, fever, and skin conditions. Bioactive constituents of Clidemia quinquenervia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Clidemia quinquenervia is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin irritations.
taxonomy: Clidemia quinquenodis belongs to the family Melastomataceae. It is a species of flowering plant native to Central and South America. The plant is classified in the kingdom Plantae. Clidemia quinquenodis further classified into the class Magnoliopsida. This plant species is also known as the "Clidemia" or "Quinine Tree".
